positions.detail: Down -99% — stop loss triggered at -50%. Close position.
Theta accelerating. Down -30%. Consider closing to limit further decay.
Down -96% — stop loss triggered at -50%. Close position.

agent_analysis:
## ELEVATED — Thursday Position Monitor

### Portfolio Status: CRITICAL DAMAGE

| Position | Stock Move | Option P&L | Status | Assessment |
|----------|-----------|------------|--------|------------|
| **DDOG CALL** | -11.1% | **-99%** | STOP_HIT | Dead. Should have been closed at -50%. Total loss. |
| **F CALL** | -9.4% | **-96%** | STOP_HIT | Dead. Same — hard stop was -50%, this blew through it. |
| **SMCI CALL** | -1.2% | **-30%** | HOLD | Warning zone. With 1 day to expiry, charm is destroying delta. |

---

### Analysis

**DDOG & F:** Both positions breached the **-50% hard stop** and are now effectively worthless (-99%, -96%). The methodology is explicit: hard stop at -50% option value. These should have been exited days ago. Whatever residual value remains (pennies) should be closed immediately — no recovery path exists with <1 day to expiry.

**SMCI:** Down -30% with stock only -1.2%. This tells me the option was likely OTM and charm decay has been the primary killer, not directional failure. On Thursday with theta accelerating, the methodology is clear: **any position below -15% should be flagged for exit — recovery through Friday is unlikely with charm acceleration.** At -30%, SMCI needs a significant move just to break even against tomorrow's final theta crush. Estimated delta is likely well below 0.20 at this point (delta stop zone approaching 0.10).

**Ensemble check:** All three calls went against thesis. No market data available to assess regime shift, but three-for-three directional failure suggests either the Monday direction signals were wrong or a macro event invalidated the thesis mid-week. The 8-week log shows a troubling CALL win rate of 0% (0/7) — this pattern demands attention in the weekly reflection.

---

### RECOMMENDATION

**Close all three positions NOW.**

DDOG and F are stop-loss violations that should have triggered days ago — take the total loss and log the execution failure. SMCI at -30% on Thursday with accelerating theta has no realistic path to the 15% Thursday target; it needs to be cut before it joins the other two at -90%+. Salvage the ~70% remaining value.

**Post-close action for reflection:** Three consecutive call losses this week, 0/7 calls over 8 weeks. The direction determination system has a severe bullish bias problem that the weekly reflection must address — the 10-signal voting system is systematically overweighting bullish signals or the confidence calibration is failing to penalize low-conviction call setups.
